step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the assessment presented by @Agent2. Additionally, another important factor often discussed but perhaps less directly addressed was ischemic hypoxia which could occur secondary to thermal injury causing vasoconstriction or tissue damage disrupting normal perfusion patterns despite adequate ventilation. This further reinforces why all three types of hypoxias together provide a comprehensive picture of potential contributing mechanisms towards mortality following severe smoke exposure.
